cards.back: A supervised learning algorithm used for classification and regression tasks, based on a tree-like model of decisions
The topmost node in the decision tree, representing the feature or attribute used to split the data
The bottom-most node in the decision tree, representing the predicted class label or output value
The process of selecting the most relevant features or attributes to split the data at each node in the decision tree
When a decision tree model is too complex and fits the training data too closely, resulting in poor performance on new, unseen data
